claim: Dogs lost from loud noises or scary situations bolt and then hide, and may remain safely in their hiding spot for several hours to several days without intervention.
stance_target: hiding phase duration after panic-trigger escape
scope_conditions: dogs that bolted from noise/fright stimulus; hiding behavior in first days post-escape
quoted_support: Dogs lost from loud noises or scary situations usually bolt and then hide. They may remain in hiding for several hours or several days. This is okay. Dogs are safe in their hiding spots. They are safe from being hit by cars or from running and getting overheated.

claim: Overfeeding a dog by well-meaning neighbors undermines humane trap efforts — dogs in survival mode must be hungry enough to take the risk of entering a trap.
stance_target: food management as prerequisite for successful trap capture of lost dog
scope_conditions: lost dogs that are sighted but reluctant to enter humane trap
quoted_support: Is your missing dog staying in one area but reluctant to enter a humane trap? 'Helpful' neighbors may be overfeeding your dog and undermining your efforts to successfully catch him. Lost dogs in survival mode need to be hungry enough to take the risk to enter a humane trap.

claim: The number one cause of lost dog death is being hit by a car, frequently resulting from too much pressure (following, calling, whistling, approaching) applied by searchers.
stance_target: pressure/chasing as primary cause of lost dog traffic mortality
scope_conditions: shy/survival-mode lost dogs under active pursuit by searchers
quoted_support: The number one cause of death of lost dogs is being hit by a car. This is often the result of too much pressure on the dog by people who are following, calling, whistling or approaching him/her.

claim: Establishing a consistent daily feeding routine at the same time and location is a prerequisite step before deploying a humane trap to capture a lost dog in survival mode.
scope_conditions: lost dogs that will not come to owner; survival mode confirmed
quoted_support: Are you getting sightings of your dog but he won't come to you? Don't take it personally! Dogs can quickly go into survival mode after being lost and may not come to anyone, including their owner. Simply feed your lost dog at the same time each day.
